checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfServices=1313492231.60 (FY2009); Revenues=3861732886.76 (FY2009) -> ~34.0% cost ratio; CostOfServices=1796926509.03 (FY2012); Revenues=4944030990.67 (FY2012) -> ~36.3% cost ratio

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?
Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-02-16 followed by 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535), amending prior year financial statement data (Assets, NetIncomeLoss quarterly breakdown for 2009-2010, Goodwill, Intangibles, etc.)
evidence.location: 10-K/A filing, cover page and comparative financial statements

evidence.quote: Multiple CORRESP and UPLOAD filings in 2011-2012 (2011-06-06, 2011-06-08, 2011-06-28, 2011-07-08, 2011-08-26, 2011-09-02, 2012-05-14, 2012-06-07, 2012-06-15) indicating SEC comment-letter correspondence around the time of the 10-K/A

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning FY2008-FY2012 provided across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, Receivables, Inventory, Goodwill, Intangibles, Allowance, Cash Flow, and Liabilities, with multiple 10-K, 10-K/A and 10-Q filings sourced (e.g., 0000950123-11-013941, 0001193125-12-121535, 0000085961-13-000007)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Restatement of previously issued financial statements evidenced by a 10-K/A (accession 0001193125-12-121535) filed roughly one month after the original 10-K (filed 2012-02-16), which re-presents full quarterly and annual comparative data for FY2009-FY2010 (e.g., Assets=4946293230.62 (2009-12-31), NetIncomeLoss quarterly breakdown for 2010 first reported only in the 10-K/A). Concurrent SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence in 2011-2012 supports a review/comment-letter process likely preceding the amendment.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535) contains Assets=4946293230.62 (2009-12-31) and quarterly NetIncomeLoss breakdown for 2010 (Q1=9776700.99, Q2=23579288.30, Q3=30686024.64, Q4=29331683.29) not present in the original 10-K filed 2011-02-15

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Allowance for doubtful accounts grew more slowly than receivables (allowance/receivables ratio fell from ~2.31% in 2009 to ~1.92%-1.99% in 2011-2012 despite a 22.7% jump in receivables in 2011), suggesting the reserve may not have kept pace with growing credit risk, potentially overstating net receivables and net income if bad debt expense was under-provided.

Goodwill increased ~64% and non-goodwill intangibles increased ~85% from FY2009 to FY2010 while revenue grew only ~5.1%, consistent with a purchase-price allocation from an acquisition; risk that a disproportionate share of purchase price was allocated to goodwill/intangibles rather than tangible/identifiable assets, or that subsequent impairment testing has been insufficiently rigorous, potentially overstating non-current soft assets.

top_signals: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535) amended the 10-K filed just one month earlier and re-presented substantial prior-period comparative data, coinciding with multiple SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD filings in 2011-2012, suggesting a restatement or SEC-prompted correction
Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of receivables declined from ~2.31% (2009) to ~1.92%-1.99% (2011-2012) even as receivables grew 22.7% in 2011, indicating potential under-reserving
Goodwill (+64%) and non-goodwill intangibles (+85%) grew far faster than revenue (+5.1%) from FY2009 to FY2010, raising purchase-accounting and impairment-testing questions
Earnings and cash flow otherwise appear reasonably volatile/consistent with revenue trends (no smoothing pattern, OCF consistently exceeds NI due to high depreciation), which is a mitigating factor
